title: Express Entry Draws - Green & Spiegel
content:
Home > Express Entry Draws

## Express Entry Draw #411 – April 15, 2026
On April 15, 2026, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) held a category based draw for French-language proficiency. In today’s draw, 4,000 invitations to apply (ITAs) were issued to candidates with a minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 419 or higher. IRCC has now invited 65,154 Express Entry candidates in 2026.
The Category-based selection invites candidates in the Express Entry pool who are eligible for a specific category established by the Minister to meet an identified economic goal. Candidates can apply for permanent residence based on criteria such as:
- Ability to communication in a specific official language
- Work experience in a specific occupation
These rounds add to general and program-specific rounds by inviting top-ranking candidates who can help us meet these specific economic goals.
To be eligibl for the French-language Proficiency Category , you must:
- Have French-language test results that show a minimum score of 7 in all 4 language abilities on the Niveaux de comp é tence linguistique canadiens
- Meet all of the requirements in the instructions for that round
Full eligibility details will be available in the instructions for each round.
IRCC applied its tie-breaking rule with the timestamp of November 14, 2025 at 07:14:25 UTC, meaning candidates with a score of 419 were invited to apply if they had submitted their Express Entry profile before the timestamp.
If you have any questions about Express Entry, Permanent Residency, or other Canadian immigration programs, please contact us to schedule a consultation.
